# v1.8.0 — Continuous open-world lineage-signal memory

This release prevents important lineage signals from being forgotten when they fall below an early broad-label naming threshold.

- Every whole-tissue, broad-class and targeted-reclustering boundary scans the complete lineage catalog.
- The selected resolution and next two higher available resolutions are audited.
- Weak coherent evidence remains a tracked `watch` signal until supported or explicitly refuted.
- Parent labels cannot restrict the candidate search space of later subclusters.
- Missing catalog coverage, unexplained-program review, large-label purity evidence or signal closure blocks completion.
- Pre-confirmation reports can include fixed-point canonical-marker expression panels across all analysis-set observations, independent of assigned labels.
- Existing v1.7 projects can migrate without fabricated evidence; completion remains blocked until historical boundaries are backfilled.
